description: Sector-specific screening controls for Nigerian insurance institutions, policyholder documents, underwriting, claims, market conduct, AML/CFT/CPF, and policyholder protection.

metadata.scope: Nigeria-only sector overlay for Insurance Sector Compliance Overlay; intended to be applied with core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from Nigerian statutes, regulations, regulator guidance, codes, directives, and sector rules available at rule-pack publication time.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: National Insurance Commission Act / NAICOM regulatory framework
NAICOM Insurance Industry Regulations, 2025
primary_sources.issuer: National Insurance Commission / Federal Republic of Nigeria

primary_sources.name: NAICOM AML/CFT/CPF Regulations for Insurance Institutions, 2022
Nigeria Data Protection Act, 2023 and GAID 2025

rules.title: NAICOM licence or insurance authorization evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for NAICOM licence, registration, authorization, or insurance institution approval evidence.

metadata.review_note: Automated result is an evidence-based screening signal only and requires qualified human/legal review before reliance.
metadata.evidence_expectation: Rule passes only when the uploaded document or document set contains matching evidence signals; absence or weak evidence should be reviewed by a human reviewer.

rules.title: Insurance KYC/CDD/AML/CFT/CPF policy commitment is documented
rules.summary: Checks for KYC, customer due diligence, AML, CFT, CPF, sanctions, or suspicious transaction controls.

metadata.review_note: Automated result is an evidence-based screening signal only and requires qualified human/legal review before reliance.
metadata.evidence_expectation: Rule passes only when the uploaded document or document set contains matching evidence signals; absence or weak evidence should be reviewed by a human reviewer.

rules.title: Policyholder disclosure and product information are documented
rules.summary: Checks for policy terms, exclusions, premium, disclosure, claims process, or policyholder information evidence.

source_basis: Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act 2018
metadata.review_note: Automated result is an evidence-based screening signal only and requires qualified human/legal review before reliance.

rules.title: Claims handling, complaints, or dispute-resolution process is documented

rules.title: No evidence of unfair or misleading insurance representation is present
